question_text: During a thunderstorm, a lightning strike occurs between a storm cloud and the ground. Which statement best explains why this happens?

options.label: A
options.text: The storm cloud becomes positively charged and the ground becomes negatively charged, so a huge electric current flows between them.

options.label: B
options.text: The storm cloud becomes negatively charged and the ground becomes positively charged, so a huge electric current flows between them to neutralise the charges.

options.label: C
options.text: Lightning occurs because the air inside the cloud is moving very fast and creates friction.

options.label: D
options.text: The storm cloud loses all its electrons, leaving it with no charge, and this causes lightning.

explanation: Storm clouds become negatively charged through friction between water droplets and ice crystals. The ground becomes positively charged by induction. The large potential difference causes a massive electrical discharge (lightning) that neutralises both charges.
